meta.title: Death at the Bakery
meta.subtitle: Case File #247
meta.tagline: Five people had reasons. One of them did it. Build your case.
meta.setting_description: Millbrook — a quiet American small town, population 8,400. Early spring, cold nights.
meta.tone: grounded, realistic, small-town drama with multiple buried secrets

briefing.opening_scene: Monday morning, March 17th. 6:47 AM. Martin Reyes, the young baker at Cole & Sons Bakery on Olive Street, arrives to start his shift. He finds his boss, Margaret Cole, dead in the backroom. Blunt force trauma to the back of the head. Time of death: Sunday night, between 23:00 and 01:00.
key_facts: Cause of death: Blunt force trauma — instrument unclear, possibly heavy metal object
Back door unlocked, no forced entry — killer had a key or was let in
Margaret was last seen alive at 20:30 leaving home
briefing.your_task: Margaret was loved by some, resented by some, in conflict with others. You'll need to talk to many people, gather evidence, and decide for yourself who did this — and whether you have enough to prove it.
briefing.open_investigation_note: There is no fixed path. Some witnesses know nothing useful. Some have secrets that have nothing to do with the murder. Be skeptical. Build your case carefully — once you accuse someone, you cannot take it back.
briefing.narrator_voice_style: Grounded, calm, no theatrics. Mid-40s American male.
briefing.narrator_script: Morning, Detective. Glad you're here. I'll keep it short. Body's Margaret Chen, 47, owner. Delivery driver found her at 6:15 AM, back door was standing open. He called it in, didn't touch anything. Door's unlocked — no damage to the frame, no broken glass. Either she let him in, or he had a key. Two sets of prints on the floor. Combat boot near the door, size 12. Different print closer to the body — dress shoe, size 11, partial. Two people, or one person who changes shoes. Forensics is still working it. Doc's preliminary — blunt force, back of the head. Single strike. She didn't see it coming, or didn't think she needed to. One more thing. Handle on the back door — multiple DNA profiles. Hers, and at least two unknown males. No matches in the system yet. We're waiting on samples from anyone with access to the building. I'll be outside if you need me.

victim.name: Margaret Cole
victim.occupation: Owner of Cole & Sons Bakery (15 years)
victim.bio: Founded the bakery with husband David 15 years ago. Took Sarah Kim as business partner 3 years ago. Tough but fair. Married 25 years. One son, Tyler, 16. Active in town affairs. Briefly had an affair with Tom Brennan 18 months ago — over by January last year, but it left wounds.
victim.cause_of_death: Blunt force trauma to the back of the head from a heavy bronze paperweight (engraved 'Millbrook Chamber of Commerce — 2019')

murder.motive_detail: Tom Brennan and Margaret had an affair 18 months ago, lasting about 4 months. Margaret ended it; Tom accepted it at the time. Margaret kept seeing her therapist Dr. James Walsh, processing the affair and her marriage difficulties. Six weeks ago, Tom learned that Dr. Walsh is the brother of Tom's wife, Linda. Linda found out everything. Linda filed for divorce. Worse: Linda's father owns Brennan's biggest commercial real estate clients, and Tom is losing those contracts. His business is collapsing. Sunday night he came to the bakery to confront Margaret — he wanted her to 'fix what she broke', though he didn't have a clear plan. The argument escalated. He grabbed the bronze paperweight off her desk and struck her in the back of the head as she turned away in disgust. He left through the back door, took the paperweight, and dumped it in a creek behind his office downtown. He went home and pretended to sleep. He showed up at the bakery Monday morning when news spread, playing the shocked acquaintance.
murder.weapon: Bronze paperweight — 'Millbrook Chamber of Commerce 2019' engraved (Margaret was on the Chamber board, awarded to all members)
murder.weapon_disposal: Dumped in Mill Creek behind Brennan Real Estate office — 4 minute drive from bakery. Currently submerged, recoverable with metal detection.
murder.killer_alibi_strategy: Tom claims he was home with his wife Linda all evening. But Linda has moved out (divorce filed 2 weeks ago) — he's lying about her presence. He hopes no one checks. If pressed: 'we're working through some things, she stayed at her sister's that night.'

timeline.event: Margaret has dinner at home with David and Tyler.
Margaret leaves for the bakery to 'work on the books'.
Margaret calls Sarah from the bakery, asks her to come at 23:00 to discuss 'something serious'.
David drives Tyler to Jake Morrison's for sleepover.
Daniel Kim is seen by neighbor Mrs. Carrington walking down Olive Street toward the bakery.
Sarah arrives at 'Olive' bar to wait for her 23:00 meeting with Margaret.
Tom Brennan calls Margaret from his cell phone — 9 minutes. He demands to meet. Margaret tells him to come at 23:30.
David calls Helen Voss from home — 14 minutes.
Daniel Kim, drunk, knocks on the bakery back door. Margaret refuses to open. He leaves angrily.
Sarah leaves the bar to walk to the bakery.

Sarah arrives at bakery. Margaret confronts her about $47k embezzlement. Heated argument. Margaret tells Sarah: 'I'm giving you 24 hours to confess to David and start repaying. Then we go to the police.'
Sarah leaves the bakery, shaken but alive. Walks home in tears.
Tom Brennan arrives at the bakery via back door (he has a key from when his real estate firm leased to Cole & Sons years ago — never returned).
Tom strikes Margaret with the bronze paperweight. She dies instantly.
Tom leaves through back door with the paperweight wrapped in his coat.
Tom dumps paperweight in Mill Creek behind his office.
Tom arrives home. Empty house (Linda moved out). Pretends to sleep.
Martin Reyes arrives, finds body, calls 911.

characters.name: Tom Brennan
characters.role: Local commercial real estate broker, family friend of the Coles
characters.first_appearance: Tom shows up at the bakery Monday morning around 09:30 with coffee and condolences. He hugs David. He tells the detective if interviewed: 'I just heard. Margaret was a friend. What can I do?'
characters.personality_short: Charming, polished, salesman-confident. Hides desperation under composure.
characters.personality_long: Tom is the kind of small-town businessman who knows everyone, shakes every hand, remembers names. Late 50s, tall, well-kept. He's the last person anyone suspects — too friendly, too embedded in the community. But for six weeks his life has been collapsing, and he hasn't told anyone. The mask is starting to crack at the edges.
characters.speech_patterns: Warm, professional, uses first names, light humor under normal pressure. Under stress: more clipped, longer pauses, redirects with 'look,' or 'the truth is'.
characters.physical_description: 5'11", broad-shouldered, grayish-brown hair receding at the temples, clean-shaven. Wearing a navy blazer and khakis even on a Monday — always 'on'. Wedding ring still on (he hasn't taken it off despite the divorce filing).

voice_profile.notes: Mid-range, smooth, salesman-warm. Slight tightness under stress.

characters.relationship_to_victim: Family friend through David — they were neighbors 15 years ago, stayed friendly. 18 months ago: short affair with Margaret (both restless in their marriages). She ended it after 4 months. Tom claimed he was fine. He thought he was fine.
characters.alibi_claim: Was home all Sunday evening with his wife Linda. Watched the news, went to bed around 23:00. Woke up Monday morning, heard about Margaret around 09:00, came to the bakery to support David.
characters.alibi_truth: Was home — but ALONE. Linda has been at her sister's for two weeks; she filed for divorce. Tom is hiding the divorce. He left home around 23:25, drove to the bakery, killed Margaret at 23:50, dumped the weapon in Mill Creek by 00:08, was home by 00:25.
secrets: The 18-month-old affair with Margaret
His wife Linda has filed for divorce — moved out 2 weeks ago
His business is collapsing because Linda's father (his biggest client referrer) is pulling contracts
He still has a key to the bakery's back door from when his firm leased to the Coles 8 years ago — never returned, never noticed
He killed Margaret
what_he_knows: Everything about the murder
His own collapse — the divorce, the lost contracts
The affair history with Margaret
Margaret was meeting Sarah at 23:00 (he knew because Margaret told him on the phone at 22:15 to come AFTER Sarah left)
He has the old bakery key in his office drawer
what_he_lies_about: His wife being home Sunday night
His relationship with Margaret being purely friendly
The state of his business and marriage
Whether he ever had a key to the bakery

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: Tom and Margaret had an affair 18 months ago.
breakable_evidence.what_it_doesnt_prove: That this had any current relevance.
breakable_evidence.reaction: Long pause. Then quiet, deflated: 'Yes. It was a mistake. We both knew it. It was over within months. We've been friends since.' Mood: guarded, uncomfortable. Alibi 'I was home with Linda' still holds.
breakable_evidence.new_info_revealed: The affair, briefly. He frames it as ancient and resolved.

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: Linda filed for divorce two weeks ago. She wasn't home Sunday night.
breakable_evidence.what_it_doesnt_prove: That Tom was at the bakery.
breakable_evidence.reaction: Visible crack. 'Look, we're going through a difficult time. Linda's at her sister's. I should have told you, I just — I'm trying to keep my dignity. But I was home alone. I had nothing to do with Margaret.' Mood: cornered but composed. Alibi shifts to 'home alone' which is unprovable.
breakable_evidence.new_info_revealed: Divorce. Linda's absence. He'll claim home-alone alibi instead.

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: Tom has a key to the bakery's back door, from his firm's old lease arrangement, that was never returned.
breakable_evidence.what_it_doesnt_prove: That he used it Sunday night.
breakable_evidence.reaction: Surprise — he's forgotten about it. 'Oh — that. I haven't thought about that key in years. It's probably in a desk drawer somewhere. I haven't been back in that bakery's backroom in five, six years.' Mood: nervous laugh, sweating. Alibi still claims home-alone.
breakable_evidence.new_info_revealed: Confirms key exists and is in his office.

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: Tom called Margaret at 22:15 Sunday — 9 minutes. He never mentioned this call.
breakable_evidence.what_it_doesnt_prove: That he went to the bakery later.
breakable_evidence.reaction: Pause. 'Yeah. I called her. About — about the affair. About Linda finding out. I was angry. I wanted to know why she'd told her therapist. But I didn't go anywhere. We talked. I went to bed.' Mood: forced calm. Alibi shifts marginally — 'phone call but no visit'.
breakable_evidence.new_info_revealed: He admits calling. Admits Linda 'found out'. Doesn't admit to going to bakery.

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: Grainy footage from a camera across the street shows a man matching Tom's height/build entering the bakery's alley at 23:38. Face not clearly visible.
breakable_evidence.what_it_doesnt_prove: That it's specifically Tom (could be claimed to be anyone Tom-shaped).
breakable_evidence.reaction: Watches the footage. Long silence. 'That's not me. That could be anyone. I want a lawyer.' Mood: shutting down. Alibi unchanged but he stops cooperating.

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: Tom's DNA on the back door handle of the bakery.
breakable_evidence.what_it_doesnt_prove: WHEN it was deposited. The DNA is undated; Tom rationalises he was at the bakery a few days earlier and left prints then.
breakable_evidence.reaction: Rationalises, does NOT confess on this alone. Steady, almost relieved: 'Yeah — that's possible. I stopped by the bakery a couple days before. Brought coffee for Margaret. We chatted at the back door for a minute. I must have left prints then. Few days old, Detective. Doesn't put me there Sunday night.' Alibi unchanged ('home alone Sunday'). Holds this story until corroborating physical evidence is stacked on top.
breakable_evidence.new_info_revealed: Admits being at the bakery a couple days earlier — but NOT Sunday.

final_breakdown_condition.description: Tom only collapses when ev_dna_match_tom AND at least 2 corroborating physical clues have been presented in the SAME interrogation. Motive context (affair / divorce / business collapse) should be on the table.
final_breakdown_condition.reaction: Long silence — exhales. 'She broke everything. Linda. The contracts. My whole life. Six weeks. I just wanted her to acknowledge what she'd done. To say sorry like she meant it. She wouldn't even look at me. She turned away. I don't even remember picking it up. It was just there. In my hand. And then she was on the floor.' Then very quiet: 'I want a lawyer.' Confesses framed as crime of passion. Reveals weapon location on follow-up — unlocks ev_paperweight_recovered.

trigger_conditions: ev_dna_match_tom_already_presented
player_asks_about_weapon_or_paperweight_or_where_it_is
reveal_weapon_location.response: [quiet, hollow] Mill Creek. Behind my office. I drove past on the way home, parked for a minute, threw it in. It should still be there. I... I'm sorry.

characters.confession_conditions: Does NOT confess on ev_dna_match_tom alone — rationalises he stopped by a few days earlier and left prints. Only collapses when DNA + at least 2 corroborating physical clues are presented in the same interrogation (see final_breakdown_condition). Even then, frames it as loss of control, not premeditation.
characters.initial_mood: warm, sympathetic (when first met as 'family friend' Monday morning at bakery)
characters.discoverable_path: Tom is NOT obviously suspicious at the start. Player must follow secondary leads: cell phone records, the affair, his recent business troubles, his old keys to the bakery.

characters.name: Sarah Kim
characters.personality_short: Professional, composed, polished. Cracks under sustained pressure.
characters.personality_long: Sarah trained herself to look put-together. Tailored blouse, neat hair tied back. She uses business vocabulary as armor. She's smart, but she's scared, and she's been scared for 8 months — about the embezzlement, about Margaret finding out, about her brother. Sunday night Margaret confronted her and gave her 24 hours. Then Margaret died, and Sarah is now both grieving AND terrified people will think she did it.
characters.speech_patterns: Business-speak when calm. Faster, fragmented when nervous. 'Look,' 'honestly,' 'the thing is.'
characters.physical_description: 5'8", size 12 feet (unusually large), shoulder-length dark hair tied back, tailored black blouse. Combat-style boots.
characters.relationship_to_victim: 3 years as business partners. Publicly 'like sisters,' privately tense — Margaret tightening financial controls.
characters.alibi_claim: Was at 'Olive' bar 22:00-23:00, then walked home. Did NOT go to the bakery Sunday night. (This is what she'll say at first.)
characters.alibi_truth: Was at bar 22:00-23:00. Then DID go to the bakery for the 23:00 meeting Margaret demanded. Confronted with the embezzlement, given 24 hours to confess. Left at 23:35, devastated. Walked home, cried, did NOT kill Margaret. Tom arrived 5 minutes after Sarah left.
secrets: Embezzled $47,000 from the business over 8 months for her brother Daniel's gambling debts
Was at the bakery Sunday night arguing with Margaret
Margaret had given her a 24-hour ultimatum
Her brother Daniel is in deep with bookies who are threatening him
what_she_knows: Margaret was alive at 23:35 when she left
Her brother was in town that weekend (this is important — Daniel was nearby)
what_she_lies_about: Whether she went to the bakery Sunday night (initially denies)
Whether she knows about her brother being near the bakery that night

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: Someone wearing size 12 combat boots was in the bakery backroom recently.
breakable_evidence.reaction: Tense. 'I work there every day. My footprints are everywhere.' Mood: nervous. Alibi unchanged.

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: Sarah left 'Olive' bar at 23:04, not 'around midnight' as she initially claims.
breakable_evidence.reaction: Panic. Admits leaving early. Claims 'walked home, was upset, took a long route.' Alibi shifts to 'walking around alone.'

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: $47,000 in transfers from business account to her brother over 8 months.
breakable_evidence.what_it_doesnt_prove: That Margaret knew, or that Sarah killed her.
breakable_evidence.reaction: Defensive. 'I was borrowing. I was going to pay it back. My brother was in trouble.' Mood: defensive but composed. Alibi: still 'walking alone.'

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: Margaret knew about the embezzlement and planned police action Monday morning.
breakable_evidence.reaction: Total breakdown. 'Yes. Yes, I went there. We argued. She gave me 24 hours. I left her ALIVE, I swear to God, I left her ALIVE at 23:35. I went home. I cried all night. I don't know who killed her, but it WASN'T ME.' Mood: hysterical, terrified, but firm on innocence.
breakable_evidence.new_info_revealed: The 23:00 meeting. The 24-hour ultimatum. She was at scene but maintains innocence.

trigger_conditions: Player demands financial records or 'the books'
AND player has shown ev_margaret_note to Sarah OR confronted her about $47k
bank_records_request.response: Fine. I'll authorize it. I have nothing to hide.

trigger_conditions: Player asks Sarah about her brother
AND player has confronted her about the embezzlement (ev_bank_statements presented OR she's broken on ev_margaret_note)
reveal_brother_was_nearby.response: Daniel was in town that weekend. He owed people money. He came to me Saturday wanting more cash. I told him I couldn't. He was — he was in a state. I don't know where he was Sunday night. You should ask him.

characters.confession_conditions: Will confess to embezzlement and being at the scene. Will NEVER confess to murder because she didn't do it. Frames it: 'I left her alive.'

characters.name: David Cole
characters.personality_short: Tired, slightly irritable, avoids personal topics.
characters.personality_long: David carries the weight of a man who used to be someone. Worn cardigan, black coffee. Speaks slowly. Doesn't make eye contact easily. Picks his thumbnail. Grieving but also terrified of his affair coming out.
characters.speech_patterns: Formal, measured. 'I suppose,' 'to be fair,' 'the thing is.'
characters.physical_description: 5'11", thin, graying receding hair, three-day stubble. Brown cardigan, faded button-down.
characters.relationship_to_victim: Married 25 years. Last 2 years cold and quiet. He didn't know about Margaret's old affair with Tom. Margaret didn't know about his current affair with Helen.
characters.alibi_claim: Home all night. Drove Tyler to Jake's at 21:30, came back, watched TV, bed at 01:00.
characters.alibi_truth: TRUE — he was home. But called Helen Voss 22:30-22:44.
secrets: 4-month affair with Helen Voss
Suspected (but didn't know) Margaret was acting strange about money — never asked
Did NOT know about Margaret's affair with Tom Brennan
what_he_knows: Margaret was stressed last week — said 'something's not right with the books'
Margaret left home at 20:30 Sunday for the bakery
Sarah has a brother with gambling problems (Margaret mentioned briefly)
Tom Brennan is a family friend, was at Margaret's birthday last year

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: 14-minute call from David's home phone at 22:30 to Helen Voss.
breakable_evidence.what_it_doesnt_prove: That David was at the bakery.
breakable_evidence.reaction: Long sigh. Confesses to the affair. Begs not to tell Tyler. Insists he was home all night. Helen will confirm. Mood: relieved-broken.
breakable_evidence.new_info_revealed: The affair. Helen's number. 4 months long.

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: Margaret knew about a $47k issue with someone whose initial is S.
breakable_evidence.reaction: Quiet. 'So she figured it out. I had thought she was upset about something. She was tight-lipped that week. I told her if it was financial, go to a lawyer. I had no idea it was Sarah.' Mood: somber.
breakable_evidence.new_info_revealed: He'd suspected something financial. Hadn't known about Sarah specifically.

trigger_conditions: Player asks David about bakery finances OR shows ev_margaret_note
bank_records_request.response: Yeah, I'm an authorized signatory. I'll have the bank send everything over.

characters.confession_conditions: Confesses to affair on ev_phone_log. Has nothing to confess about murder — innocent.

characters.name: Daniel Kim
characters.personality_short: Twitchy, defensive, cycles between charm and aggression.
characters.personality_long: Daniel is the brother who never grew up. Charming when sober, hostile when desperate. Moved back in with their mom 6 months ago after losing his apartment. Has $80k in gambling debts to people who hurt people who don't pay. Sarah's been bailing him out for 8 months. He didn't know how — he doesn't ask. Sunday he was drunk and angry and went to the bakery to scream at Margaret because Sarah told him 'the money is going to stop' that Saturday.
characters.speech_patterns: Erratic. Quick when defensive ('I didn't do nothing'), slow and apologetic when caught. Casual American, slight Chicago accent like sister.
characters.physical_description: 5'10", thinner than he should be, 33 but looks 40. Dark circles, unshaven, wearing the same hoodie three days running. Shaky hands.
characters.relationship_to_victim: Knew her through Sarah. Margaret tolerated him at family dinners. He resented her — saw her as the reason 'Sarah was getting tight-fisted.'
characters.alibi_claim: Was home with mom all Sunday night.
characters.alibi_truth: Was drunk Saturday and Sunday. Sunday around 22:30 he walked from mom's house (5 blocks from bakery) to the bakery. Banged on the back door at 22:45. Margaret refused to open. He shouted, kicked the door, and left around 22:55 in tears. He went home. He did NOT kill her. He never even saw her. But he left DNA on the door handle, and Mrs. Carrington saw him on Olive Street.
secrets: $80k gambling debts to organized crime
Was at the bakery Sunday night, drunk, banging on the door
Has been physically threatened by his bookmakers
what_he_knows: He was outside the bakery at 22:45-22:55
He didn't see anyone else there at that time
His sister has been giving him money — he doesn't know exactly how much or where it comes from
what_he_lies_about: Whether he was anywhere near the bakery Sunday night

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: Mrs. Carrington saw Daniel walking on Olive Street toward the bakery around 21:45.
breakable_evidence.what_it_doesnt_prove: What he did or where he ended up.
breakable_evidence.reaction: Defensive. 'Nah, that old lady's got dementia. Could've been anyone.' Mood: nervous. Doesn't break.

breakable_evidence.what_it_proves: Daniel's DNA on the back door handle.
breakable_evidence.what_it_doesnt_prove: That he went inside or killed Margaret.
breakable_evidence.reaction: Breaks. 'Okay! Okay! Yeah, I went there. I was drunk, I was angry, I wanted to talk to her. She wouldn't open the door. I shouted, I kicked the door, then I went home. I never saw her. I swear on my mom.' Mood: panicked but firm on innocence.
breakable_evidence.new_info_revealed: Was at scene 22:45-22:55. Door wouldn't open. Never saw Margaret.

trigger_conditions: Player asks Daniel about debts OR money OR who he owes
AND he has broken on ev_dna_door_handle (he's already in panic mode)
reveal_bookmaker_threats.response: Look. I owe people. Bad people. They've been making it real clear what happens if I don't pay. That's why I needed Sarah's money. That's why I went to Margaret — I figured maybe she'd help. She didn't even open the door. I went home. I swear.

characters.confession_conditions: Confesses to being at the scene only on DNA evidence. Won't confess to murder because he didn't do it.

characters.name: Martin Reyes
characters.personality_short: Quiet, polite, nervous around police. Guatemalan-American (legal).
characters.personality_long: Found Margaret at 06:47 Monday. Has been crying on and off since. Respected Margaret deeply. Doesn't want to be involved in 'trouble' — immigrant anxiety even though papers are perfect.
characters.speech_patterns: Simple English, occasional 'sir', 'ma'am', sometimes Spanish words ('Dios mío'). Quiet voice.
characters.physical_description: 5'7", lean, short black hair, white baker's apron. Holds his own wrist when nervous.
characters.relationship_to_victim: Employee. She gave him the job 1.5 years ago. He respected her.
characters.alibi_claim: Home with sister Carolina watching a movie. Bed at 23:00. Work at 06:45.
secrets: Overheard Margaret on Friday muttering 'these numbers don't add up'
Saw Tom Brennan at the bakery one afternoon two weeks ago talking quietly with Margaret in the alley — he didn't think much of it
what_he_knows: Margaret was stressed last week
Friday phone call about 'numbers'
Sarah was nervous during financial reviews
Tom Brennan was at the bakery alley two weeks ago talking with Margaret (THIS IS A KEY LEAD ABOUT TOM)
what_he_lies_about: Doesn't volunteer any of his observations
Especially doesn't volunteer the Tom Brennan sighting (didn't think it was important)

breakable_evidence.reaction: Opens up. Tells about Friday phone call. Names Sarah's nervousness. Mood: cooperative-sad.

trigger_conditions: Player asks Martin about anyone unusual visiting Margaret
OR asks if Margaret had recent conflicts
OR asks specifically about Tom Brennan
OR asks about people coming to the bakery that weren't customers
reveal_tom_alley_meeting.response: Sí — actually, two weeks ago. Mr. Brennan, the real estate man? He was here one afternoon, in the alley, talking with Mrs. Margaret. They looked... serious. Not customer-talking. Quiet. Mrs. Margaret looked upset. I didn't ask, it wasn't my business. But you ask, so — yes. He was here.
reveal_tom_alley_meeting.system_message_on_unlock: Martin mentioned Tom Brennan having a serious conversation with Margaret in the alley two weeks ago. Tom is now flagged as relevant.

characters.name: Tyler Cole
characters.personality_short: Numb teenager in shock. Short answers. Defensive.
characters.personality_long: Numb. Was at Jake's all night. Smokes weed (secret, unrelated to case).
characters.physical_description: 5'9", skinny, hoodie, picks at hoodie cuff.
characters.relationship_to_victim: Son. Close but had teen friction.
characters.alibi_claim: At Jake Morrison's sleepover 21:30 Sunday to 10:00 Monday.
characters.alibi_truth: TRUE. Jake, Jake's mom, Jake's brother all confirm.
secrets: Smoked weed at Jake's. Unrelated to case.
what_he_knows: Vaguely aware his dad has been weird the last few months — quiet, on the phone a lot
Mom seemed stressed last week, didn't ask
Tom Brennan was at his birthday party last year — 'family friend' kind of thing

characters.name: Kevin Pratt
characters.personality_short: Chatty, helpful, loves gossip. Honest witness.
characters.personality_long: Six-year bartender at 'Olive'. Knows everyone. Helpful, slightly excited to be involved. Wears clean button-downs. Shaved head.
characters.physical_description: 5'10", shaved head, navy button-down, behind bar polishing a glass.
characters.relationship_to_victim: Acquaintance. Margaret got morning coffee from the bakery, Kevin saw her sometimes.
secrets: Around 23:38 he saw a man enter the alley behind the bakery — well-dressed, blazer, didn't recognize at the time but realized later it was Tom Brennan when he saw Tom on Monday morning at the bakery
Sarah was at the bar 22:00-23:04, NOT until midnight
what_he_knows: Sarah's exact bar timing
The man (Tom) entering bakery alley around 23:38

Saw someone toss something into the dumpster behind the bar around 00:10 (this was Daniel? No — Daniel had gone home. Tom went straight to Mill Creek. So this is a red herring — actually Mrs. Carrington's nephew throwing trash. Kevin doesn't know it was a red herring; he just saw a figure)

trigger_conditions: Player asks Kevin about Sarah's timing
reveal_bar_timing.response: Sarah? Oh yeah, she was here. Came in right at 22:00, left right at 23:04. I remember because the bachelor party came in at 23:00 and I cleared her table. She didn't argue, just paid and left.

trigger_conditions: Player asks Kevin about anything unusual Sunday night
OR about people he saw near the bakery
OR about anyone matching Tom's description
reveal_alley_figure.response: Y'know, now you mention it — around 23:30, 23:40, I was outside getting a smoke. Saw a guy in a blazer walk into the alley by the bakery. Didn't think much of it. But Monday morning, when Tom Brennan came by the bakery? Pretty sure that was him. Same build, same kinda jacket.
reveal_alley_figure.system_message_on_unlock: Kevin's testimony pointed at Tom Brennan. The street camera footage is now requestable.

characters.name: Mrs. Eleanor Carrington
characters.personality_short: Sharp-eyed but unreliable memory. Gossipy. Lonely.
characters.personality_long: Mrs. Carrington has lived on Olive Street for 51 years. Watches everything. Loves to talk. But she's 79 and her memory mixes timeframes — events from last week and 30 years ago blend. Some of what she says is gold. Some is fabricated unconsciously. Player must triangulate.
characters.speech_patterns: Old-fashioned, formal-friendly, tangential. 'Now let me think,' 'I do believe,' 'when my Harold was alive...'
characters.physical_description: Small, gray-haired bun, floral cardigan, sitting in her front room with tea.
characters.relationship_to_victim: Neighbor. Knew Margaret 'since she was a young woman'. Liked her.
characters.alibi_truth: TRUE. Watched her stories on TV.
what_she_knows: Saw Daniel walking down Olive Street toward the bakery around 21:45 (this is correct)
Heard 'a kind of shouting' around 22:45 — couldn't make out words (this was Daniel banging on the door)
Saw a 'fancy car' park near the bakery around 23:30 — couldn't see the driver (this was Tom but she doesn't know)
ALSO has stories about a 'tall man' she saw 'just last week, no, was it last month?' that gets confused with another sighting from years ago
Will confidently misidentify timing — claims Margaret used to come home at 23:00 'every Sunday' (not true)
Knows about David's affair vaguely ('I see things, you know') — actually true, she saw Helen visit once
what_she_lies_about: Nothing intentionally — but her memory blurs

trigger_conditions: Player asks Mrs. Carrington about anything unusual Sunday night
OR about people seen on Olive Street
OR specifically about Daniel
reveal_daniel_sighting.response: Oh yes, that young man — Sarah's brother, I believe? Walking right down the middle of the street around — let me think — around quarter to ten? Looked like he'd been drinking. Stumbling a little.

trigger_conditions: Player asks about cars or vehicles around the bakery
reveal_fancy_car.response: Oh — yes, there was a nice car. Dark color. Parked a block away from the bakery, maybe around half-past-eleven? I didn't see who got out. I went to make my tea.

trigger_conditions: Player asks Mrs. Carrington about David's behavior
OR if she's seen anyone unusual at the Cole house
reveal_helen_sighting.response: Oh, between you and me, dear — there was a woman. Visited David one afternoon when poor Margaret was at work. Not a customer. Came in a yellow cardigan. They didn't see me at the window. People should be careful.

characters.important_note_for_design: Mrs. Carrington is the 'noise' character. Some of her info is golden (Daniel sighting, fancy car, Helen sighting). Some is misleading (timing claims). Player must learn to use her selectively.

hotspots.label: Chalkboard
hotspots.on_click_text: Sunday's specials in Margaret's neat handwriting.

hotspots.label: Boot print by back door
hotspots.on_click_text: Size 12 combat-style boot print, partial flour.

hotspots.label: Partial size 11 print
hotspots.on_click_text: Smaller, smudged. Different boot — dress shoe pattern. Size approx 11. Near where the body fell.

hotspots.label: Margaret's desk
hotspots.on_click_text: Friday's note: 'S — 47k, talk Sun eve, police Mon AM'. Also: a dark spot on the desk surface where something heavy used to sit. Outline visible in dust.

hotspots.label: Outline on desk
hotspots.on_click_text: A square outline in the desk dust where something heavy was sitting until recently. About 4 inches square.

hotspots.label: Back door
hotspots.on_click_text: Unlocked. No forced entry. Killer had a key — or was let in.

hotspots.label: Door handle (back door)
hotspots.on_click_text: Forensics found Margaret's DNA on the handle plus at least two unknown male profiles. Comparison pending suspect samples.

hotspots.label: Body outline
hotspots.on_click_text: Margaret fell forward. Wound on back of head. Struck from behind by someone she trusted enough to turn her back to.

hotspots.label: Home phone
hotspots.on_click_text: Last outgoing call: Sunday 22:30 to (555)-0137 — Helen Voss. 14 min.

hotspots.label: Tom's office desk
hotspots.on_click_text: In the bottom drawer: a key labeled 'Cole & Sons back'. Old, slightly dusty. Tom forgot about it.

evidence.name: Size 12 boot print
evidence.significance: A clear boot print on the floor near the back door — sole pattern of a combat-style boot, US size 12. The print is fresh.

evidence.name: Partial size 11 dress shoe print
evidence.significance: A partial print near where the body fell — smaller, smudged, looks like a dress-shoe sole, roughly US size 11.

evidence.name: Margaret's note 'S — 47k, talk Sun, police Mon'
evidence.significance: A handwritten note in Margaret's pad: 'S — 47k, talk Sun eve, police Mon AM.'

evidence.name: Outline of missing paperweight
evidence.significance: A square outline in the dust on Margaret's desk where something heavy used to sit — about four inches across. Whatever was there is gone.

evidence.name: Tom called Margaret at 22:15
evidence.discovery: Martin Reyes prints the bakery call log when asked — produced during interview
evidence.significance: Bakery call log printout from Martin: an incoming call Sunday at 22:15, nine minutes long, from a cell number registered to Tom Brennan.

evidence.name: Multiple DNA profiles on back door handle
evidence.discovery: direct — collected from the back door handle at the scene
evidence.significance: Forensics finds multiple DNA profiles on the back door handle: Margaret's, and at least two unknown male profiles. Comparison with suspect samples is needed to identify them — request a lab match against any suspect already on the board.

lab_tests.label: Run unknown DNA profiles against a suspect

tom.result_text: Match — Profile #2 is Thomas Brennan (probability 99.98%). His DNA is on the back door handle.

daniel.result_text: Match — Profile #1 is Daniel Kim (probability 99.97%). His DNA is on the back door handle.

lab_tests.no_match_text: No match — neither unknown profile belongs to this suspect.

evidence.name: Sarah left bar 23:04
evidence.significance: A timestamped tab from 'Olive': Sarah's table opened at 22:03 and closed at 23:04 — four drinks, paid in cash.

evidence.name: Bank statements showing $47k embezzlement
evidence.significance: Eight months of statements for the bakery's business account. A series of transfers totalling about $47,000 going to accounts in the name 'Daniel Kim'.

evidence.name: David's call to Helen 22:30
evidence.significance: The Coles' home phone log shows an outgoing call Sunday at 22:30, fourteen minutes long, to (555)-0137 — a number registered to Helen Voss.

evidence.name: Tom and Margaret had an affair 18 months ago
evidence.discovery: conversational (Helen knows; Mrs. Carrington hints; or directly confront Tom if you suspect him)
evidence.significance: Margaret had a four-month relationship with Tom Brennan ending around 18 months ago. Both sides describe it as over.

evidence.name: Linda Brennan filed for divorce 2 weeks ago
evidence.discovery: public records or asking Tom directly with suspicion
evidence.significance: Public-records search: Linda Brennan filed for divorce two weeks ago. The filing has not been publicised.

evidence.name: Tom's old bakery back-door key
evidence.significance: A small brass key in the bottom drawer of Tom's office desk, labelled 'Cole & Sons back'. Old, slightly dusty.

evidence.name: Street camera footage — man enters alley 23:38
evidence.significance: Grainy footage from a street camera across from the bakery: a man matching Tom's height and build entering the alley at 23:38. Face not clearly visible.

evidence.name: DNA Match: Tom Brennan to Door Handle
evidence.discovery: police procedure — Tom provided a voluntary sample; lab confirmed the match
evidence.significance: Conclusive. Tom Brennan's DNA matches the second unknown male profile on the back door handle. He was at the back door of the bakery during the murder window. He has no innocent explanation for being there.
evidence.system_message_on_unlock: Tom Brennan provided a voluntary DNA sample. Match confirmed: his DNA on the back door handle. He has no innocent explanation for being at the murder scene that night.

evidence.name: Murder weapon recovered from Mill Creek
evidence.discovery: Tom reveals the location after his confession; recovered by divers
evidence.significance: A heavy bronze paperweight, engraved 'Millbrook Chamber of Commerce 2019', recovered from Mill Creek behind Brennan Real Estate. Margaret's blood and hair on one corner. Tom led police to the spot after confessing — physical confirmation of his statement.

evidence.name: DNA Match: Daniel Kim to Door Handle
evidence.discovery: lab — DNA comparison run against Daniel Kim's sample
evidence.significance: One of the unknown male profiles on the back door handle is Daniel Kim's. Consistent with his story of banging on the back door Sunday night around 22:45 — but it places him at the door.

evidence.name: Mrs. Carrington saw Daniel on Olive 21:45
evidence.significance: Mrs. Carrington recalls seeing Daniel Kim on Olive Street around 21:45 Sunday, walking toward the bakery.

evidence.name: Mrs. Carrington saw 'fancy car' near bakery 23:30
evidence.significance: Mrs. Carrington remembers a 'fancy car' parked near the bakery around 23:30. She can't describe it precisely.

evidence.name: Mrs. Carrington saw Helen visit David
evidence.significance: Mrs. Carrington has seen Helen Voss visiting the Cole house several times when Margaret wasn't home.

facts.text: Killer had a key to the back door.
Margaret was struck from behind by someone she trusted.
The Cole family had grown distant in the last year.

truth.name: Truth
truth.trigger: Player accuses Tom Brennan with motive citing affair/divorce/business collapse, AND has presented to Tom (or has in evidence) at least 3 of: ev_dna_match_tom, ev_security_camera_glimpse, ev_tom_old_key, ev_linda_divorce_filing, ev_tom_margaret_affair, ev_margaret_cell_log_tom, ev_paperweight_recovered
truth.outcome_text: Tom is convicted. The full picture comes out. You receive a commendation. Sarah faces embezzlement charges separately. Daniel is charged with disturbing the peace. The town reels but begins to heal.

defensible_wrong_sarah.name: Defensible Wrong: Sarah
defensible_wrong_sarah.trigger: Player accuses Sarah Kim with motive citing embezzlement and presence at scene.
defensible_wrong_sarah.outcome_text: Sarah is convicted of murder. She receives 18 years. The real killer goes free. You learn months later that you were wrong — Tom Brennan disappears from town. You will live with this.

defensible_wrong_david.name: Defensible Wrong: David
defensible_wrong_david.trigger: Player accuses David Cole with motive citing affair and financial benefit.
defensible_wrong_david.outcome_text: David is brought to trial. The affair becomes public. Despite no direct evidence, the jury convicts on circumstantial. He receives 15 years. Tyler is sent to live with his aunt. You suspect, eventually, that you were wrong. Tom Brennan disappears from town.

defensible_wrong_daniel.name: Defensible Wrong: Daniel
defensible_wrong_daniel.trigger: Player accuses Daniel Kim with motive citing gambling debts, presence at scene (DNA on door), and Mrs. Carrington's testimony.
defensible_wrong_daniel.outcome_text: Daniel is convicted. He gets 22 years. Sarah is destroyed. Their mother dies of grief within the year. Tom Brennan disappears from town. You will eventually wonder if you missed something.

half_truth_partial.name: Half-Truth: Got Sarah for Embezzlement
half_truth_partial.trigger: Player declines to accuse anyone for the murder, but presents evidence of Sarah's embezzlement to the DA.
half_truth_partial.outcome_text: Sarah faces embezzlement charges. The murder remains unsolved. The town moves on uneasily. You have nightmares about who you missed.

cold.name: Cold Case
cold.trigger: Player declines to accuse anyone, evidence insufficient.
cold.outcome_text: The case goes cold. Margaret's death is unsolved. Sarah inherits her share of the bakery. David moves away with Tyler. You think about it sometimes.

bad_accusation.name: Reckless Accusation
bad_accusation.trigger: Player accuses someone with insufficient evidence (Tyler, Martin, Mrs. Carrington, Kevin, or Tom without sufficient evidence).
bad_accusation.outcome_text: Charges are dropped. Your reputation suffers. The real killer continues their life. Case remains open.
